living-abstract.essence: Information classification system with type-specific processing strategies
living-abstract.purpose: Enable strategic knowledge processing by classifying information into five types, each requiring distinct cognitive operations
living-abstract.ability: Identify incoming information as Procedural, Analogous, Conceptual, Evidence, or Reference
Select the correct digestion strategy for each classified type
Invest processing effort proportionally: C > A > P > E > R
living-abstract.principle: Different information types require different cognitive operations -- one-size-fits-all fails
What stays in the brain matters more than what goes in -- always digest what you consume
Conceptual knowledge forms the structural skeleton; other types attach to it
Not everything needs deep encoding -- Reference is deliberately shallow
